mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable federal funds rate keeps the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, encouraging continued capital flow into equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the effect disappears (t-stat < 1.5) once controlling for prior 60-day SPX momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized consumer and residential investment strength, a combination that historically leads to broad-based earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone carries the same predictive power.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3m annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3m annualized growth > 5%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show no statistically significant return premium over single-series triggers (interaction term coefficient p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained deceleration in industrial production growth (INDPRO) below trend signals supply-side capacity strain and impending margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is muted during early-cycle recoveries.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration episodes in late-cycle windows fail to produce below-median forward 60d returns relative to full-sample average, or rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects real-time labor demand strength, and positive payroll surprises are absorbed by equity markets within a week, producing short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns as risk appetite improves.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints show no significant difference from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth accelerates above 2.5%, markets price in higher probability of future rate hikes, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation periods show forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than baseline (p > 0.05), invalidating the compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based demand slowdown that compounds risk of recession beyond what either series indicates alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS 3m annualized growth < 1%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce more negative forward 60d returns than single-indicator triggers alone (interaction coefficient p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (rising FEDFUNDS), a simultaneous uptick in unemployment (UNRATE) signals that policy tightening is beginning to bite the labor market, amplifying downside equity risk beyond either signal individually and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3m trend AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes during tightening cycles show no significant negative skew in forward 5d returns relative to tightening periods without the UNRATE uptick (p > 0.10).
